description: Lazy load — fault-driven on-demand abstraction loading. Catches NULL_CAP on manifest-registered slots, delegates to Tunnel.Fetch (method 4) to download and install the lump, then retries the faulting CALL transparently. Evict is a no-op stub until a Memory abstraction exists.

methods.name: Load
methods.description: Fault-driven load. Delegates to Tunnel.Fetch to download and install the requested lump from the IDE. CR2 (memory write-GT) must be set by the caller (the fault handler). Returns 0 on success, non-zero on timeout/CRC/size error.
inputs: CR2: Memory write-GT (write-perm buffer, set by fault handler)
DR1: NS slot token (identifies the requested abstraction)
DR2: expected lump size in words
comments: LOAD CR1, CR6[0]  — load Tunnel GT from c-list slot 0 into CR1
CALL CR1, 4       — call Tunnel.Fetch (method 4); DR1=token, DR2=expected_words, CR2=write-GT

methods.name: Prefetch
methods.description: Hint-driven load. Identical to Load — calls Tunnel.Fetch which is idempotent (already-resident lumps are a no-op at the IDE bridge). Returns 0 if installed or already resident.
inputs: CR2: Memory write-GT (write-perm buffer, set by caller)
DR2: expected lump size in words
outputs: DR0: 0 = installed or already resident | non-zero = error
comments: LOAD CR1, CR6[0]  — load Tunnel GT from c-list slot 0 into CR1
CALL CR1, 4       — call Tunnel.Fetch (method 4); idempotent — already-resident is fine

methods.name: Evict
methods.description: Unload a cold abstraction to reclaim memory. Stub — returns 0 immediately. Full implementation requires a Memory abstraction (not yet defined). GT and c-list are preserved; the next CALL on this slot triggers Load again.
inputs: DR1: NS slot token (not used by stub)
comments: RETURN  — stub: no Memory abstraction available yet; DR0 = 0 (no-op success)
